<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Zeta values — ζ at integers</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  table.plain td.num{font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;color:var(--cyan)}
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>

rawBodyPrefix:
← Index
1 Basel 2 Even values 3 Apéry 4 Negative values 5 Functional eq.

Zeta values — $\zeta$ at integers

The Riemann zeta function $\zeta(s)=\sum_{n=1}^\infty n^{-s}$ conceals one of mathematics' great treasures: at integer arguments, its values reveal $\pi$, rational numbers, and deep irrationality theorems. From Euler's 1734 proof that $\zeta(2)=\pi^2/6$ to Apéry's 1978 bombshell that $\zeta(3)$ is irrational, and from the trivial zeros at negative even integers to the functional equation connecting left and right half-planes, this page uncovers the arithmetic of $\zeta$ at integers.

blocks.html:
1. The Basel problem: $\zeta(2)=\pi^2/6$

The Basel problem, posed by Pietro Mengoli in 1650, asks for the exact value of the series $1 + \tfrac{1}{4} + \tfrac{1}{9} + \tfrac{1}{16} + \cdots$. Jakob Bernoulli confirmed it converges but could not find the sum. In 1734, the twenty-seven-year-old Euler stunned the mathematical world by announcing the answer: $\pi^2/6$.

Euler's product proof

Euler's key insight was to treat $\sin(\pi x)$ as an "infinite polynomial" with roots at $x=\pm 1, \pm 2, \pm 3,\ldots$ Factoring it analogously to a finite polynomial with leading term $\pi x$ (since $\sin(\pi x)/(\pi x)\to 1$ as $x\to 0$) gives the Weierstrass product:

$$\sin(\pi x) = \pi x \prod_{n=1}^{\infty}\!\left(1 - \frac{x^2}{n^2}\right).$$

Dividing both sides by $\pi x$:

$$\frac{\sin(\pi x)}{\pi x} = \prod_{n=1}^{\infty}\!\left(1 - \frac{x^2}{n^2}\right).$$

Now expand both sides in powers of $x^2$. The Taylor series gives $\sin(\pi x)/(\pi x)=1-\tfrac{(\pi x)^2}{6}+\tfrac{(\pi x)^4}{120}-\cdots$, so the coefficient of $x^2$ is $-\pi^2/6$. The product expansion gives

$$\prod_{n\ge 1}\!\left(1-\frac{x^2}{n^2}\right) = 1 - \left(\sum_{n=1}^\infty\frac{1}{n^2}\right)x^2 + \cdots$$

Matching coefficients of $x^2$:

$$\boxed{\zeta(2) = \sum_{n=1}^{\infty}\frac{1}{n^2} = \frac{\pi^2}{6}.}$$
Rigour note. Euler's argument assumed without proof that $\sin(\pi x)$ could be treated like a finite polynomial for coefficient-matching purposes. Rigorous justification via uniform convergence of infinite products came later. The result is nonetheless correct.

params.title: Basel partial sums vs $\pi^2/6$

layout.html: Cyan: partial sum $S_N=\sum_{k=1}^{N}1/k^2$. Yellow dashed: $\pi^2/6\approx 1.6449$. The series converges, but slowly — a reminder that the exact value $\pi^2/6$ is non-trivial.

params.hint: drag slider to add more terms

params.title: Basel partial sums vs $\pi^2/6$

layout.html: Cyan: partial sum $S_N=\sum_{k=1}^{N}1/k^2$. Yellow dashed: $\pi^2/6\approx 1.6449$. The series converges, but slowly — a reminder that the exact value $\pi^2/6$ is non-trivial.

blocks.html:
See also
  • Dirichlet series basics
Used in
Zeta values — ζ at integers · Even values ζ(2n) via Bernoulli numbers

2. Even values: $\zeta(2n)$ via Bernoulli numbers

Euler did not stop at $\zeta(2)$. By extending his product argument — or equivalently, by using the Bernoulli generating function — he derived a complete formula for all even positive-integer values. The statement requires the Bernoulli numbers $B_{2n}$:

$$\boxed{\zeta(2n) = (-1)^{n+1}\frac{B_{2n}\,(2\pi)^{2n}}{2\,(2n)!}, \qquad n=1,2,3,\ldots}$$

The Bernoulli numbers $B_{2n}$ are the rational coefficients in the generating function $\tfrac{t}{e^t-1}=\sum_{k\ge 0}B_k \tfrac{t^k}{k!}$, with $B_0=1$, $B_2=\tfrac{1}{6}$, $B_4=-\tfrac{1}{30}$, $B_6=\tfrac{1}{42}$, $\ldots$ The formula confirms that every $\zeta(2n)$ is a rational multiple of $\pi^{2n}$.

$n$$\zeta(2n)$Closed form$B_{2n}$
11.6449…$\pi^2/6$$1/6$
21.0823…$\pi^4/90$$-1/30$
31.0173…$\pi^6/945$$1/42$
41.0041…$\pi^8/9450$$-1/30$
51.0009…$\pi^{10}/93555$$5/66$
Why rational times $\pi^{2n}$? The functional equation (§5) relates $\zeta(1-2n)$ to $\zeta(2n)$, and the left side is rational (a Bernoulli number). Each transcendental factor of $\pi$ on the right must be balanced by the power of $2\pi$ in the formula — the Bernoulli numbers supply the precise rational correction.

params.title: Even zeta values: formula vs numerical sum

layout.html: Bar chart: $k^{-2n}$ for $k=1\ldots 30$. Notice how terms decay faster for larger $n$ — the series converges much faster than $\zeta(2)$.

params.hint: pick $n$, compare closed form to partial sum

params.title: Even zeta values: formula vs numerical sum

layout.html: Bar chart: $k^{-2n}$ for $k=1\ldots 30$. Notice how terms decay faster for larger $n$ — the series converges much faster than $\zeta(2)$.

params.hint: pick $n$, compare closed form to partial sum

blocks.html:
See also
  • Bernoulli numbers and their generating function
Used in
Zeta values — ζ at integers · Odd values and Apéry's constant ζ(3)

3. Odd values and Apéry's constant $\zeta(3)$

While every even positive-integer value of $\zeta$ admits the elegant formula above, odd values are a mystery. We know $\zeta(3)\approx 1.20206$ from numerical computation, but no closed form involving $\pi$ is known — and there are strong heuristic reasons to believe none exists.

Apéry's theorem (1978)

In 1978, Roger Apéry announced at a conference that $\zeta(3)$ is irrational. The audience was initially skeptical — the proof came with no prior publications and used an obscure sequence. Within months, mathematicians including Alfred van der Poorten verified the proof. It rests on the Apéry numbers $a_n = \sum_{k=0}^{n}\binom{n}{k}^2\binom{n+k}{k}^2$ and a rapid rational approximation to $\zeta(3)$:

$$\zeta(3) = \frac{5}{2}\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}\frac{(-1)^{n+1}}{n^3\binom{2n}{n}}.$$

The denominators of the partial sums grow slower than the denominators of any rational approximation to $\zeta(3)$ — a contradiction if $\zeta(3)$ were rational. The proof establishes irrationality but says nothing about transcendence.

What about $\zeta(5), \zeta(7), \ldots$? In 2001, Tanguy Rivoal proved that infinitely many odd zeta values are irrational, and in 2022 Wadim Zudilin showed at least one of $\zeta(5),\zeta(7),\zeta(9),\zeta(11)$ is irrational. Whether each individual odd value is irrational — let alone whether any has a $\pi^{2k+1}$-type closed form — remains completely open.

params.title: Apéry series convergence

layout.html: Pink dots: partial sums of the Apéry series $\tfrac{5}{2}\sum_{k=1}^{N}\tfrac{(-1)^{k+1}}{k^3\binom{2k}{k}}$. Yellow dashed: $\zeta(3)\approx 1.20206$. The series converges much faster than $\sum k^{-3}$.

params.title: Apéry series convergence

layout.html: Pink dots: partial sums of the Apéry series $\tfrac{5}{2}\sum_{k=1}^{N}\tfrac{(-1)^{k+1}}{k^3\binom{2k}{k}}$. Yellow dashed: $\zeta(3)\approx 1.20206$. The series converges much faster than $\sum k^{-3}$.

blocks.html:
4. Negative-integer values and trivial zeros

The Dirichlet series $\sum n^{-s}$ converges only for $\operatorname{Re}(s)>1$. But Riemann proved in 1859 that $\zeta(s)$ extends to a meromorphic function on all of $\mathbb{C}$, with a single simple pole at $s=1$ with residue $1$. This analytic continuation assigns finite values to all non-pole points, including the negative integers.

The values at negative integers are determined by the Bernoulli numbers:

$$\zeta(-n) = -\frac{B_{n+1}}{n+1}, \qquad n=0,1,2,3,\ldots$$

For instance:

$\zeta(0)=-\tfrac{1}{2},\quad \zeta(-1)=-\tfrac{1}{12},\quad \zeta(-2)=0,\quad \zeta(-3)=\tfrac{1}{120},\quad \zeta(-4)=0,\ldots$

The zeros $\zeta(-2)=\zeta(-4)=\zeta(-6)=\cdots=0$ are the trivial zeros of $\zeta$. They arise because $B_3=B_5=B_7=\cdots=0$ (odd Bernoulli numbers past $B_1$ vanish), so $\zeta(-2k)=-B_{2k+1}/(2k+1)=0$ for $k\ge 1$.

Not actual sums. Writing $1+2+3+\cdots=-\tfrac{1}{12}$ is a shorthand for $\zeta(-1)=-\tfrac{1}{12}$, where $\zeta$ is the analytically continued function. The ordinary divergent series has no value; the Riemann zeta provides a consistent extension of the summation concept to regions where the series diverges.

layout.html: Bars show $\zeta(-n)$ for $n=0,1,\ldots,12$. Values at even $n\ge 2$ are exactly zero (trivial zeros). Note the rapid growth of $|\zeta(-n)|$ for odd $n$.

blocks.html:
See also
  • Bernoulli numbers — generating function and values
  • Euler's formula $\zeta(2n)$ from the Bernoulli perspective
  • Analytic continuation
Used in
Zeta values — ζ at integers · The functional equation ξ(s) = ξ(1−s)

5. The functional equation $\xi(s)=\xi(1-s)$

Riemann's 1859 memoir introduced a completed zeta function that absorbs the gamma factor encoding the Archimedean place:

$$\xi(s) = \pi^{-s/2}\,\Gamma\!\left(\tfrac{s}{2}\right)\zeta(s).$$

The profound symmetry is the functional equation:

$$\xi(s) = \xi(1-s).$$

This reflects the critical strip $0\le\operatorname{Re}(s)\le 1$ about the line $\operatorname{Re}(s)=\tfrac{1}{2}$. Written out as a relation between $\zeta(s)$ and $\zeta(1-s)$:

$$\zeta(1-s) = 2\,(2\pi)^{-s}\cos\!\left(\tfrac{\pi s}{2}\right)\Gamma(s)\,\zeta(s).$$

This is the bridge between the two worlds: values for $\operatorname{Re}(s)>1$ (where the Dirichlet series converges) and values for $\operatorname{Re}(s)<0$ (where Bernoulli numbers live).

Recovering the Bernoulli formulas

At $s=2n$ (positive even integer), $\cos(\pi n)=(-1)^n$, $\Gamma(2n)=(2n-1)!$, and $\zeta(2n)=(-1)^{n+1}B_{2n}(2\pi)^{2n}/(2(2n)!)$. Substituting into the right side and collecting powers of $2\pi$ gives $\zeta(1-2n)=-B_{2n}/(2n)$, the Bernoulli-number formula for the negative values.

Non-trivial zeros. A zero of $\zeta$ in $0<\operatorname{Re}(s)<1$ is a non-trivial zero. The functional equation forces zeros to come in pairs $\{\rho, 1-\rho\}$. The Riemann Hypothesis asserts all such $\rho$ satisfy $\operatorname{Re}(\rho)=\tfrac{1}{2}$; it remains unproven.

params.title: Functional equation: value pairing $\zeta(s)\leftrightarrow\zeta(1-s)$

layout.html: Number line showing the real axis. Yellow dot: $s$. Pink dot: $1-s$. The midpoint $s=1/2$ (the critical line) is always at the center of the reflection.
